headings: overlay_set(name, x, y, height, width, colors)

descriptions.connect: Creates a bridge between two nodes.
descriptions.disconnect: Removes a specific bridge between two nodes.
descriptions.is_connected: Checks if a direct or indirect connection exists between two nodes.
descriptions.nodes_clear: Removes all connections from the board.
descriptions.node: Creates a node object from a string name or integer ID. This is useful for storing a node reference in a variable.
descriptions.dac_set: Sets the output voltage for a specific DAC channel.
descriptions.dac_get: Reads the currently set voltage for a DAC channel.
descriptions.adc_get: Reads the voltage from a specific ADC channel.
descriptions.gpio_set: Sets the output state of a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_get: Reads the state of a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_set_dir: Sets the direction of a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_get_dir: Reads the direction of a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_set_pull: Configures the internal pull resistor for a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_get_pull: Reads the pull resistor configuration of a GPIO pin.
descriptions.gpio_set_read_floating: Enable or disable special floating detection when reading GPIO inputs. **These are all enabled by default.**
descriptions.gpio_get_read_floating: Returns whether floating-read detection is enabled for a GPIO pin.
descriptions.pwm: Sets up and starts a PWM signal on a GPIO pin.
descriptions.pwm_set_duty_cycle: Changes the duty cycle of an existing PWM signal.
descriptions.pwm_set_frequency: Changes the frequency of an existing PWM signal.
descriptions.pwm_stop: Stops the PWM signal on a GPIO pin.
descriptions.overlay_set: Creates or updates a graphic overlay.
descriptions.overlay_shift: Moves an overlay by a relative offset. Wraps around edges.
descriptions.overlay_place: Moves an overlay to a specific absolute position. Wraps around edges.
descriptions.overlay_set_pixel: Sets a single pixel directly (convenience wrapper).
descriptions.overlay_serialize: Returns the current state of all overlays as a JSON string.
descriptions.ina_get_voltage: Reads the shunt voltage in Volts.
descriptions.ina_get_bus_voltage: Reads the bus voltage in Volts.
descriptions.oled_print: Displays text on the OLED screen. It can print strings, numbers, and custom Jumperless types.
descriptions.oled_show: Refreshes the OLED display to show the latest changes.
descriptions.oled_connect: Connects the I2C lines to the OLED display.
descriptions.oled_disconnect: Disconnects the I2C lines from the OLED display.
descriptions.oled_set_text_size: Set the default text size for all subsequent `oled_print()` calls.
descriptions.oled_get_text_size: Get the current default text size.
descriptions.oled_copy_print: Enable or disable copying Python `print()` output to the OLED display in real-time.
descriptions.oled_get_fonts: Get a list of all available font families.
descriptions.oled_set_font: Set the current font family by name. The font will remain active until changed.
descriptions.oled_get_current_font: Get the name of the currently active font family.
descriptions.oled_load_bitmap: Load a bitmap file into the internal bitmap buffer.
descriptions.oled_display_bitmap: Display a bitmap on the OLED.
descriptions.oled_show_bitmap_file: Convenience function that loads and displays a bitmap in one call.
descriptions.oled_get_framebuffer: Get a copy of the current OLED framebuffer as a bytes object.
descriptions.oled_set_framebuffer: Set the entire OLED framebuffer from bytes or bytearray.
descriptions.oled_get_framebuffer_size: Get the dimensions and size of the framebuffer.
descriptions.oled_set_pixel: Set a single pixel on the OLED.
descriptions.oled_get_pixel: Get the color value of a single pixel.
descriptions.probe_read: Reads the pad currently being touched by the probe.
descriptions.probe_button: Reads the state of the buttons on the probe.
descriptions.get_switch_position: Gets the current probe switch position.
descriptions.set_switch_position: Manually sets the probe switch position.
descriptions.check_switch_position: Checks the probe switch position using current sensing and updates the internal state.
descriptions.probe_autoconnect: Gets or sets whether the probe DAC auto-connects on the routable buffer.
descriptions.clickwheel_get_position: Gets the raw clickwheel position counter.
descriptions.clickwheel_reset_position: Resets the clickwheel position counter to 0.
descriptions.clickwheel_get_direction: Gets the current clickwheel direction event.
descriptions.clickwheel_get_button: Gets the current clickwheel button state.
descriptions.get_net_name: Gets the name of a specific net.
descriptions.set_net_name: Sets a custom name for a net.
descriptions.get_net_color: Gets the color of a net as a 32-bit RGB value.
descriptions.get_net_color_name: Gets the color name of a net as a human-readable string.
descriptions.set_net_color: Sets the color of a net by name, hex string, or RGB values.
descriptions.set_net_color_hsv: Sets the color of a net using HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value) color space. Automatically detects whether you're using normalized (0.0-1.0) or full-range (0-255) values based on the hue parameter.
descriptions.get_num_nets: Gets the number of currently active nets.
descriptions.get_num_bridges: Gets the total number of bridges (connections).
descriptions.get_net_nodes: Gets all nodes in a net as a comma-separated string.
descriptions.get_bridge: Gets information about a specific bridge.
descriptions.get_net_info: Gets comprehensive information about a net as a dictionary.
descriptions.get_num_paths: Gets the number of routing paths currently in use.
descriptions.get_path_info: Gets detailed information about a specific routing path.
descriptions.get_all_paths: Gets all routing paths as a list of dictionaries.
descriptions.get_path_between: Queries the routing path between two specific nodes.
descriptions.pin.value: For INPUT: Reads the current pin state (0 or 1).
descriptions.send_raw: Sends raw data to core2 for direct chip control.
descriptions.change_terminal_color: Sets the terminal text color using 256-color ANSI codes.
descriptions.cycle_term_color: Cycles through the terminal color palette.
descriptions.force_service: Forces immediate execution of a specific system service by name.
descriptions.force_service_by_index: Forces immediate execution of a specific system service by index (faster than name lookup).
descriptions.get_service_index: Gets the index of a service by name for use with `force_service_by_index()`.
descriptions.switch_slot: Switches to a different connection slot.
descriptions.context_toggle: Toggles the connection context between `global` and `python` modes.
descriptions.context_get: Gets the current connection context name.
descriptions.get_state: Returns the entire board state as a formatted JSON string. This includes nets, power settings, and GPIO configuration.
descriptions.set_state: Applies a board state from a JSON string or, if ``from_wokwi`` is True, from a
descriptions.jfs.listdir: Returns a list containing the names of the entries in the directory given by `path`.
descriptions.jfs.exists: Check if a file or directory exists.
descriptions.jfs.stat: Get status of a file or directory.
descriptions.jfs.open: Open a file and return a corresponding file object.
descriptions.file.read: Read `size` bytes from the file. If `size` is omitted or negative, the entire file is read.
descriptions.file.write: Write the given string or bytes `data` to the file. Returns the number of bytes written.
descriptions.file.close: Close the file. A closed file cannot be read or written to.
descriptions.file.size: Return the total size of the file in bytes.
descriptions.file.available: Return the number of bytes available to be read from the current position to the end of the file.
descriptions.file.name: Returns the name of the file.
descriptions.help: Displays a comprehensive list of all available functions and constants in the `jumperless` module.
descriptions.nodes_help: Displays a detailed reference for all available node names and their aliases.

connect.0: Force exactly 0 duplicates (removes any existing duplicate paths)
connect.node1: Node to connect. 1-60, D0-A7, TOP_RAIL, BOTTOM_RAIL, GND, GPIO_1-GPIO_8, UART_TX/RX, ADC0-4, DAC0/1, ISENSE_PLUS/MINUS
connect.node2: Node to connect. 1-60, D0-A7, TOP_RAIL, BOTTOM_RAIL, GND, GPIO_1-GPIO_8, UART_TX/RX, ADC0-4, DAC0/1, ISENSE_PLUS/MINUS
connect.-1: Just add the connection without managing duplicates (standard behavior)
connect.1+: Force exactly N duplicates (adds or removes connections to reach that count)
connect.0+: Force exactly N duplicates (0 makes a single path)

disconnect.node1: Node to disconnect from. 1-60, D0-A7, TOP_RAIL, BOTTOM_RAIL, GND, GPIO_1-GPIO_8, UART_TX/RX, DAC0/1, ADC0-4, ISENSE_PLUS/MINUS
disconnect.node2: Set to -1 to disconnect all from node1.

dac_set.channel: The DAC channel to set. DAC0, DAC1, TOP_RAIL, BOTTOM_RAIL.
dac_set.save: If `True` (default), the setting is saved to the config file.

gpio_set_pull.pull: `1` for PULLUP, `-1` for PULLDOWN, `0` for NONE.

gpio_set_read_floating.enabled: `True` to enable floating-read behavior, `False` to disable.

pwm.frequency: The PWM frequency in Hz (0.001 to 62500000). Defaults to 1000.
pwm.duty_cycle: The duty cycle from 0.0 to 1.0. Defaults to 0.5.

pwm_set_frequency.frequency: The new frequency in Hz (0.001 to 62500000).

overlay_set.name: Unique string identifier for the overlay.
overlay_set.x: Starting column (**1-30**). Matches breadboard column labels.
overlay_set.y: Starting row (**1-10**). 1-5 = Top half (A-E), 6-10 = Bottom half (F-J).
overlay_set.colors: List of 32-bit integer colors (0xRRGGBB). Can be a flat list or 2D list (rows).

overlay_clear.name: The identifier of the overlay to remove.

overlay_shift.name: Overlay identifier.
overlay_shift.dx: Column delta (e.g., 1 for right, -1 for left).
overlay_shift.dy: Row delta (e.g., 1 for down, -1 for up).

oled_print.size: Text size (0=small scrolling, 1=normal, 2=large). If -1 or omitted, uses the default size set by `oled_set_text_size()`. Defaults to 2.

oled_clear.show: If `True` (default), automatically calls `oled_show()` after clearing. Set to `False` for animations to avoid flashing between frames.

oled_set_font.name: ['Eurostile', 'Jokerman', 'Comic Sans', 'Courier New', 'New Science', 'New Science Ext', 'Andale Mono', 'Free Mono', 'Iosevka Regular', 'Berkeley Mono', 'Pragmatism']

oled_display_bitmap.width: Bitmap width in pixels (ignored if using loaded bitmap)
oled_display_bitmap.height: Bitmap height in pixels (ignored if using loaded bitmap)

oled_set_framebuffer.data: Framebuffer data (must be correct size for display)

probe_read.blocking: If `True` (default), the function will wait until a pad is touched. If `False`, it returns immediately.

probe_button.blocking: If `True` (default), waits for a button press. If `False`, returns the current state immediately.
probe_button.consume: Each button press is detected only once - ideal for menu navigation or one-shot actions

probe_autoconnect.enable: `True` to enable auto-connect, `False` to disable. If omitted, returns the current state without changing it.

clickwheel_get_direction.consume: If `True` (default), clears the direction after reading (one-shot detection). If `False`, the direction persists until consumed.

get_net_name.netNum: The net number (0 to number of nets - 1).

set_net_name.name: The new name string. Pass empty string or `None` to reset to default.

set_net_color.color: Color as a name ("red", "blue", "pink") or hex string ("#FF0000", "0xFF0000").
set_net_color.r: If providing RGB values directly, pass them as separate arguments.
set_net_color.g: If providing RGB values directly, pass them as separate arguments.
set_net_color.b: If providing RGB values directly, pass them as separate arguments.

set_net_color_hsv.s: Saturation value. Defaults to maximum saturation (255) if not provided or negative.
set_net_color_hsv.v: Value/brightness. Defaults to 32 (reasonable LED brightness) if not provided or negative.

get_bridge.bridgeIdx: The bridge index (0 to number of bridges - 1).

get_num_paths.include_duplicates: If `True` (default), count all paths including duplicates. If `False`, count only primary (non-duplicate) paths.

get_path_between.node: Any routable node to read from

run_app.appName: The name of the app to run (e.g., "File Manager", "I2C Scan").

change_terminal_color.color: Color index (0-255), or -1 to reset to default

cycle_term_color.reset: If `True`, reset to start of color sequence
cycle_term_color.step: Color increment step (defaults to the last one you set)

switch_slot.slot: The slot number to switch to (0-7).

set_state.json: A JSON string representing the state (same format as returned by
set_state.from_wokwi: If `True`, interpret `json` as a Wokwi diagram.json and
set_state.print_paths(): Prints all resolved paths between nodes.
set_state.print_crossbars(): Prints the raw state of the crossbar matrix.
set_state.print_chip_status(): Prints the status of the CH446Q chips.

jfs.mkdir.path: The path of the new directory.

jfs.rmdir.path: The path of the directory to remove.

jfs.remove.path: The path of the file to remove.

jfs.stat.path: The path of the file or directory.

jfs.open.mode: The mode in which the file is opened. Defaults to `'r'`.
jfs.open.'w': Write (creates a new file or truncates an existing one).

file.seek.0: Seek from the start of the stream (default). Use `jfs.SEEK_SET`.
file.seek.1: Seek from the current position. Use `jfs.SEEK_CUR`.
file.seek.2: Seek from the end of the stream. Use `jfs.SEEK_END`.
